section.heading: Legal actions by debt collectors

subsection.heading: Venue
subsection.chapeau: Any debt collector who brings any legal action on a debt against any consumer shall—

paragraph.content: in the case of an action to enforce an interest in real property securing the consumer’s obligation, bring such action only in a judicial district or similar legal entity in which such real property is located; or

paragraph.chapeau: in the case of an action not described in paragraph (1), bring such action only in the judicial district or similar legal entity—

subparagraph.content: in which such consumer signed the contract sued upon; or
in which such consumer resides at the commencement of the action.

content.p: Nothing in this subchapter shall be construed to authorize the bringing of legal actions by debt collectors.

section.sourceCredit: (Pub. L. 90–321, title VIII, § 811, as added Pub. L. 95–109, Sept. 20, 1977, 91 Stat. 880.)

note.heading: Effective Date
note.p: Section effective upon the expiration of six months after Sept. 20, 1977, see section 819 of Pub. L. 90–321, as added by Pub. L. 95–109, set out as a note under section 1692 of this title.
